title: 10 best herbal teas for asthma
intro_desc: Herbal teas for asthma are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate asthma symptoms. These teas contain natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties, which help relax the airways and improve breathing. Some popular herbal teas for asthma include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, turmeric, and fenugreek. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation, while ginger tea has been shown to improve lung function. Licorice root tea can help soothe irritated airways, and turmeric tea contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and relieves asthma symptoms. Fenugreek tea, on the other hand, can help reduce asthma-related coughing and wheezing. Drinking these teas regularly can significantly improve the quality of life for asthma sufferers by reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European goldenrod teas helps with asthma because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making breathing easier. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids also have natural bronchodilatory effects, which can help to relax the airway muscles and increase airflow. Additionally, goldenrod has been shown to have a gentle expectorant action, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, further alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: European Goldenrod tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common symptom of asthma, thereby alleviating symptoms.

Bronchodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in European Goldenrod tea have been shown to relax bronchial muscles, making it easier for air to flow into and out of the lungs, reducing bronchospasm and wheezing associated with asthma.

Mucolytic properties: European Goldenrod tea contains mucolytic compounds that help break down mucus, reducing its thickness and viscosity, allowing for easier clearance from the lungs and alleviating symptoms such as coughing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the European Goldenrod plant contain a compound called sesquiterpene lactone, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce bronchial spasms in patients with asthma.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the European Goldenrod plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to relax airway muscles and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried solidago virgaurea flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized pot or teapot.
Pour boiling water over the goldenrod flowers and steep for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, allowing it to steep for another few minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have a natural bronchodilatory effect, helping to relax the airways and reduce inflammation. The camphor and thymol present in thyme tea also possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections that exacerbate asthma symptoms. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of thyme tea can help to relieve coughing and wheezing, providing relief from asthma attacks and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can help alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Flowers: Contain essential oils that help open up airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchial constriction.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the thyme leaves in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the herbal thyme tea hot, with honey and lemon if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier. The tea also contains expectorants that loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for more efficient airflow. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum has been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to open up constricted airways and relieve wheezing and coughing.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant and bronchodilator that helps to relieve respiratory congestion and ease breathing in people with asthma.
Cineole: Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and make it easier for allergens to trigger an asthma attack.
Phellandrene: Phellandrene is a natural expectorant that helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain a high concentration of expectorant compounds that help relieve respiratory congestion and coughing associated with asthma.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, being underground stems that store nutrients, contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator or air-drying method for 2 weeks.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried eucalyptus leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a large glass jar or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Transfer the strained tea to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.
Serve the tasmanian blue gum tea hot or chilled, with honey or lemon added to taste, and enjoy its decongestant properties within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena teas helps with asthma because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways. The tea's calming properties also help to soothe spasms and ease coughing, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, verbena's natural expectorant properties can help loosen mucus and promote a healthy expulsion from the body, further relieving respiratory symptoms associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Verbena tea helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate asthma symptoms, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: The tea's natural expectorants help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, which can help alleviate congestion and coughing associated with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Verbena tea contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation in the lungs, helping to calm and soothe irritated airways, and potentially reducing the severity of asthma attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Verbena roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm bronchial passages in people with asthma.
Leaves: The leaves of the verbena plant contain compounds that have been shown to relax airway muscles, reducing inflammation and constriction associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems from your garden or store-bought dried form for use in tea.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the herb's active compounds to dissolve and release flavors.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or mug, discarding solids and leaving the tea.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or chilling in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes for a refreshing cold brew.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with asthma because it contains mucilages, which are natural thickening agents that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs. When brewed into a tea, marshmallow root can help to relax airway muscles, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by asthma attacks. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow teas may help to reduce bronchial spasms and ease breathing, providing relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tea's mucilage content helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by asthma attacks.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins in marshmallow tea may help reduce airway inflammation, a key characteristic of asthma.
Expectorants: Marshmallow tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the respiratory tract, making it beneficial in tea form for alleviating asthma symptoms.

Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant is rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate breathing difficulties associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers or dried herbs in the morning.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb mixture in a tea infuser or teapot for 5 to 7 minutes with boiling water.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with asthma because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce airway inflammation and constriction. The compounds present in licorice root tea, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing bronchial hyperresponsiveness and improving lung function. Additionally, licorice teas may also help soothe irritated airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in licorice tea have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
Licoricidin: This compound has been found to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, such as histamine and leukotrienes, which are involved in the development of asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing bronchial spasms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant are used to make a sweet-tasting tea that can help soothe and calm respiratory issues, including asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in amounts suitable for your desired tea strength and dosage.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve and mingle with the water.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids, and enjoy within 30 minutes for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coltsfoot teas helps with asthma because it contains saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate bronchospasms. The tea's expectorant properties also help loosen and clear out mucus and other debris from the lungs, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. Additionally, coltsfoot's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. By soothing and relaxing the airways, coltsfoot teas can provide natural relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Coltsfoot tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can help alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Mucolytic properties: Coltsfoot tea contains compounds that break down mucus, making it easier for the body to expel and helping to relieve congestion and coughing associated with asthma.

Bronchodilatory properties: The active compounds in coltsfoot tea can help relax and dilate airways, making it easier for air to pass through and relieving symptoms of bronchospasm, which is common in people with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in relieving bronchial congestion and inflammation.
Seeds: Contain mucilages that help soothe and calm the airways, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves and flowers of tussilago farfara, ensuring a minimum of 2 tablespoons per cup.
Combine harvested material with boiling water in a large teapot or infuser, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and sediment.
Add honey to taste if desired, stirring well to dissolve before serving immediately.
Allow tea to cool slightly before refrigerating and consuming within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have a bronchodilatory effect, which means they help to relax the airways and improve breathing. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herb also reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, the tea has been shown to decrease mucus production and coughing, further alleviating symptoms of asthma. As a result, drinking eastern purple coneflower teas can provide relief from the symptoms of asthma and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Eastern Purple Coneflower tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and airway damage in people with asthma.

Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant compounds, such as iridoid glycosides, help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it effective in relieving asthmatic symptoms.
Bark: The bark has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including asthma, due to its ability to calm bronchial spasms and reduce congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from sustainable sources.
Combine one teaspoon of dried flowers and leaves with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Enjoy the warm tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop teas helps with asthma because its natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing congestion and making breathing easier. The herb's expectorant properties also help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, further relieving symptoms of wheezing and coughing. Additionally, hyssop tea has been shown to dilate bronchial passages, allowing for more efficient airflow and improving lung function, providing natural relief for asthma sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of hyssop tea help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
Bronchodilatory properties: Hyssop tea's bronchodilatory properties help to relax the muscles in the airways, which can constrict during an asthma attack, allowing for easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate bronchial spasms.
Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop plant contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in loosening mucus and reducing asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried hyssopus officinalis flowers into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the hyssop flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a spoon or a strainer and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to combine with the herbal tea liquid.
Pour the herbal hyssop tea into a cup and serve hot or let it cool to drink later.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet basil teas helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways. The essential oils present in sweet basil tea also work to relax the bronchial muscles and open up air passages, making it easier for air to flow in and out of the lungs. This natural relaxation of the bronchi allows for improved lung function, providing relief from asthma symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Essential Oils: The essential oils present in sweet basil tea, such as eugenol and linalool, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate asthma symptoms.

Antioxidants: Sweet basil tea contains a rich source of antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the lungs, thus reducing the severity of asthma attacks.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene present in sweet basil tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve lung function, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used in traditional medicine, basil roots are believed to help reduce inflammation and open airways, making them effective for relieving asthma symptoms.
Leaves: Sweet basil leaves have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather one quart of fresh or dried ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers.
Combine the basil leaves and flowers with two cups of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for eight to ten minutes to release the aromatic oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Serve the sweet basil tea hot, garnished with additional fresh basil leaves if desired.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat asthma with herbal teas is by consuming licorice root and ginger teas. Licorice root tea helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Ginger tea, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate asthma symptoms by relaxing the muscles surrounding the airways. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating these teas into your asthma treatment plan, as they can interact with certain medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal teas include bronchitis and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal teas such as licorice root, thyme, and eucalyptus are often used to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Licorice root tea can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Thyme tea has antimicrobial and expectorant properties that can help clear mucus and reduce coughing. Eucalyptus tea can also help clear the airways by reducing inflammation and acting as an expectorant. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment for any medical condition.
